g.data-narration: Welcome to Introduction to Data Structures. Let's explore how computers organize and manage data efficiently.
A data structure is a specialized format for organizing, processing, and storing data for efficient access and modification.
g.text: A specialized format for organizing, processing, retrieving, and storing data.
Enables efficient access and modification of data in computer memory.

g.data-narration: Understanding data structures improves program performance dramatically. They provide organization, reusability, and abstraction.
Data structures are classified by organization into Primitive and Non-Primitive types, and by memory allocation into Static and Dynamic.
Primitive data structures are basic types provided by programming languages: integers, floats, characters, and booleans.
Linear structures arrange elements sequentially with unique predecessors and successors. Non-linear structures represent hierarchical relationships.
Static data structures have fixed size at compile time, while dynamic structures can grow and shrink during execution.
g.text: Fixed size at compile time • Memory allocated at compile
Size changes during execution • Memory allocated at runtime

g.data-narration: Arrays store elements in contiguous memory locations, enabling fast random access using indices. They are fixed-size structures.
Linked lists store elements in nodes with pointers, allowing dynamic size and efficient insertion/deletion at any position.
Stacks follow LIFO (Last In First Out) principle, while queues follow FIFO (First In First Out) for element ordering.
Trees are hierarchical structures with a root node and child nodes. Graphs are networks of nodes connected by edges.
Abstract Data Types (ADTs) define data by behavior rather than implementation, hiding internal details from users.
g.text: Defines data by its behavior (operations) rather than implementation.

g.data-narration: Time complexity measures algorithm execution time as input size grows. Common complexities include O(1), O(n), and O(log n).
